summary: Iteration 119 — Resource Reassignment: 5-min undo window + multi-service (Travel/Car Rental/Hotel). BACKEND: 12/12 NEW pytest PASSED (test_iteration119_reassign_undo_multi.py). Verified undo happy path (fresh reassign → POST /reassignments/{id}/revert returns reverted_event_id + new_event_id + affected_count), double-revert 400 'Event has already been reverted', unknown-event 404, expired (>5min) event returns 400 with 'expired/window', snapshot restoration via orders' reassignment_history containing both the swap and revert entries (with is_revert_of pointing at original event_id). GET /reassignments now returns revertable boolean + age_seconds + revert_window_minutes=5 on the envelope; old events (age_seconds>300) correctly have revertable=false. Car rental dry-run reassign between two cars of same operator succeeds and returns make/model snapshot; cross-operator rejected 400. Hotel dry-run reassign between 2 rooms of same hotel succeeds; cross-hotel rejected 400. Unknown service_type (cinema) rejected 400. FRONTEND: Travel → Management → Vehicles tab renders 2 replace-vehicle-btn-* buttons; clicking opens the NEW GENERIC ReplaceResourceModal (screenshot-confirmed) with adaptive 'Replace Vehicle' title, BEING REPLACED card (Replacement Bus Alpha RE-999-XX, 40 seats), Replacement vehicle dropdown, Reason pills (Breakdown pre-selected), Note textarea, Preview impact button — exactly matching the travel serviceType preset. data-testids 'replace-resource-modal', 'replace-resource-select', 'replace-preview-btn', 'replace-confirm-btn', 'replace-undo-btn' all present in source (grep-verified on ReplaceResourceModal.jsx). replace-car-btn-<id> exists on CarRentalManagement.jsx line 269, replace-room-btn-<id> exists on RoomCard.jsx line 212. Full undo-click round-trip and Car/Hotel modal open verification were blocked mid-run by an admin re-login timeout (likely cookie/auth race after operator logout, not a product issue) — the selectors and adaptive-label logic are sound based on source review + working Travel flow.

issues: Dashboard stats endpoint returns 500 — pre-existing from iter117/118, not in iter119 scope but still pollutes console

action_items: Optional: Main agent quick self-test of the undo COUNTDOWN UI — click a fresh Travel Replace → Preview → Confirm → verify '05:00' amber timer + 'Undo this reassignment' button → click undo → verify toast 'Undone. N booking(s) reverted.' and modal closes. Backend undo flow is fully verified via pytest (12/12).

Optional: Main agent spot-check Car Rental (/management/car-rental → Cars tab → click replace-car-btn) and Hotel (/management/hotel → pick hotel → Rooms → click replace-room-btn) to visually confirm the generic modal adapts to 'Replace Car' / 'Replace Room' labels.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/resource_reassignments.py L581-726 (revert endpoint): clean implementation — reuses _build_snapshot/_get_affected_orders, marks original with reverted_by_event_id + reverted_at, emits is_revert_of on new event and in history_entry, re-uses _fan_out_notifications. Minor: the notification title on a revert is the same 'Your <noun> has been changed' template — consider a dedicated 'Reassignment undone' title so customers aren't confused by two consecutive 'changed' notifications.
routes/resource_reassignments.py L612-622 (expiration check): correctly handles both naive and tz-aware datetimes. Good.

SERVICE_SPECS['hotel'].compat_fields = ['hotel_id'] is declared but never actually used — the hotel-specific check is hard-coded at L412-417. Either remove the unused field or switch to a data-driven check so future services (events/banquet) can plug in.
_resolve_operator_id resolves room→hotel→operator_id but CarRental spec compat_fields=['operator_id'] and cars carry operator_id directly — correct. Good generalization.

ReplaceResourceModal.jsx: the 5-min countdown interval should be cleared in the close/cancel path to avoid a dangling setInterval if the user closes the modal manually — verify useEffect cleanup covers the 'X' close case.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% — Travel modal end-to-end render confirmed visually; Car/Hotel modal open not visually re-confirmed this run but selectors verified in source

seed_data_creation: Two fresh reassignment events created for travel during pytest: one for undo happy-path (reverted immediately), one direction-detection. Also admin-scope dry-runs against car_rental (Toyota Corolla↔Toyota Land Cruiser, Hilton operator) and hotel (two Hilton Hotel rooms). All dry-runs; no order mutation for car/hotel.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter119 undo + multi-service all green at API layer. For next UI-only retests: (1) use operator@test.com for Travel (2 vehicles exist, cross-swappable); (2) use admin@test.com for Car Rental / Hotel because operator@test.com does not own cars or rooms. (3) The new selector is [data-testid='replace-resource-modal'] — old 'replace-vehicle-modal' is DELETED. (4) After a fresh reassign, the 5-min countdown MUST start from '05:00' — if it starts at '00:00' or immediately shows '00:xx', the backend age_seconds is being used incorrectly.
rca of the issue: N/A — no new bugs surfaced this iteration.
